title: The TCP/IP Protocol Suite: A Four-Layer Model
description: A visual representation of the four layers of the TCP/IP model, showing the key protocols residing at each layer and how data flows from an application down through the network for transmission.
relationships: Data is encapsulated from the top down: from segments (Transport) to packets (Internet) to frames (Network Access).
Each layer provides services to the layer above it and uses services from the layer below it.
The Internet Layer is the unifying layer that enables internetworking across different physical networks.
